rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN J. RUSSELL VS. WEISBERG & MILLER, AN ENTITY STRUCTURE UNKNOWN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY RUSSELL'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion to quash is denied. The Court has specific jurisdiction over defendant. The suit arises out of defendant's specific contact with the jurisdiction in a manner similar to that found in Daar & Newman v. VRL Intern., 129 Cal.App.4th 482, 493 (2005). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DORA SU-ORTIZ VS. UNION BANCAL CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Unopposed Mtn For Unopposed Mtn For Order Of Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Approving Proposed Ntc To Class
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS DORA SU-ORTIZ, ERIASI AKRIDGE'S MOTION For Order Of Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Approving Proposed NOTICE To Class. Denied without prejudice. (A) The Court is unable to estimate whether either (1) the total settlement sum of $600,000 or (2) the net which may be allocated to class members (total less fees, costs, administrators' fees and other deductions) is a fair estimate of the liability, costs and risks of the litigation. In particular, The Court was not able to determine the number of hours or wages at issue. (B) The notice does not provide an easily understandable estimate of the recovery a class member may obtain from the settlement. Even if the precise dollar figure cannot be estimated at this time, both the Court and class members require a means to estimate an approximate number. Further motions in connection with this settlement are to be noticed before Judge Karnow, who may at the time be in a department different from 302. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA A. JAGIELLO VS. SUSAN ELISABETH CHRISTMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT BARBARA JAGIELLO'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the UCL and breach of contract claims and overruled as to the conversion claim. These are demurrers to the fourth amended cross complaint. The earlier order of this Court dated April 26, 2012 likely contained an error in not deleting 1 (c) from the form offered by counsel, in that the prior demurrer was overruled on this claim. Accordingly the demurer to the conversion claim is overruled. Contrary to the court prior order, Christman fails to specifically allege the amount of money paid in actual reliance on Jagiello's alleged unfair conduct. Christman, therefore, lacks standing to bring the UCL claim. Christman now alleges she voided the contract and in the absence of another alleged contract between the parties the breach of contract claim cannot lie. Instead of complying with the court's earlier order, the putative contract claim appears to invoke, instead, other types of claims such as B&P § 6148. At this late stage in the multiple demurrer proceedings, it does not appear that any of these deficiencies can be cured. The request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MONA FIELD et al VS. DEBRA BOWEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees; Memo P/A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 5, INTERVENOR CALIFORNIA INDEPENDENT VOTER PROJECT Notice Of Motion For Attorneys' Fees. Judge Woolard of this Court denied plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction. The Court of Appeal affirmed that ruling and held that plaintiffs were unlikely to prevail on the merits as a matter of law. Field v. Bowen, 199 Cal.App.4th 346 (2011). This Court subsequently granted judgment in defendants' favor. Thereafter, the Legislature enacted AB 1413. Now two groups of parties seek attorneys' fees: the plaintiffs, and interveners. Both motions are denied. Intervenor seeks $243,279.50 in fees against Plaintiffs under CCP § 1021.5. (They also seek an additional $43,000 for work just on the fees motions. Interveners' Reply, filed July 25, 2012 at 10.) It is true that prevailing defendants may recover fees under that statute. DiPirro v. Bondo Corp., 153 Cal.App.4th 150, 199 (2007) ("An award of attorney fees pursuant to section 1021.5 is available if a party defends an action '" 'primarily to advance' "' a public interest '"'rather than personal interests.'") Three basic criteria are required to support an award of attorneys' fees under section 1021.5:(1) the action resulted in the enforcement of an important right affecting the public interest; (2) a significant benefit was conferred on the general public or a large class of persons; and (3) the necessity and financial burden of private enforcement were such as to make the award appropriate. Abouab v. City and County of San Francisco 141 Cal.App.4th 643, 663 (2006). For reasons stated above I reject Plaintiffs' suggestion that they, and not interveners, are prevailing. I reject as inapposite the analogies made to Title VII and FEHA cases. See Plaintiffs' Opposition to Interveners' Motion For Attorney Fees, filed July 13, 2012 at pp. 10 et seq. The central issue here is the extent to which plaintiffs were simply pursuing private rights (with perhaps an incidental public interest). Adoption of Joshua S., 42 Cal.4th 945 (2008), for example, held that a § 1021.5 fee award may not be imposed on a litigant who did nothing to adversely affect the public interest, but simply lost an important appellate case in the course of pursuing his or her private rights. See also, Wilson v. San Luis Obispo Democratic Central Comte., 192 Cal.App.4th 918, 925 (2011); Serrano v. Stefan Merli Plastering Co., Inc., 52 Cal.4th 1018 (2011). Here, plaintiffs were seeking to protect what they perceived to be their own rights and to avoid what they perceived to be disadvantages to them as potential candidates, under the legislative regime they challenged. To be sure, this of necessity implicated statewide issues and matters of public import, but that that does not cast plaintiffs outside the protection of Adoption of Joshua S. It follows that attorney fees are not awardable. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: MONA FIELD et al VS. DEBRA BOWEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Declaration And Attys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS MONA FIELD, RICHARD WINGER, STEPHEN CHESSIN, JENNIFER WOZNIAK, JEFF MACKLER, RODNEY MARTIN'S Motion For Declaration And AttORNEys Fees. Judge Woolard of this Court denied plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction. The Court of Appeal affirmed that ruling and held that plaintiffs were unlikely to prevail on the merits as a matter of law. Field v. Bowen, 199 Cal.App.4th 346 (2011). This Court subsequently granted judgment in defendants' favor. Thereafter, the Legislature enacted AB 1413. Now two groups of parties seek attorneys' fees: the plaintiffs, and interveners. Both motions are denied. Plaintiffs' Motion Citing CCP § 1021.5, plaintiffs seek nearly $2 million in attorney's fees claiming that they were the catalyst for AB 1413. Graham v. DaimlerChrysler, 34 Cal.4th 553 (2004). Plaintiffs claim they prompted the Secretary of State and Legislature to stop disenfranchising voters who cast write in votes. Under the catalyst theory, attorney fees may be awarded even when litigation does not result in a judicial resolution if the defendant changes its behavior substantially because of, and in the manner sought by, the litigation¿In order to be eligible for attorney fees under section 1021.5, a plaintiff must not only be a catalyst to defendant's changed behavior, but the lawsuit must have some merit¿" Graham, 34 Cal.4th at 560-561 (emphasis added). Here, the lawsuit had no merit. Plaintiffs are not a "successful party" under CCP § 1021.5. AB 1413 did not provide Plaintiffs with any relief: it was, in terms of the relief sought in this case, unnecessary in light of the Court of Appeal's holding that Election Code § 8606 barred the casting of a write-in vote. AB 1413 merely substituted clearer statutory language to achieve the same result. Further, the enactment of AB 1413 was not the primary relief sought. Compare, Schmier v. Supreme Court, 96 Cal.App.4th 873, 878 (2002), quoting Maria P. v. Riles, 43 Cal.3d 1281, 1291-92 (1987). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating And Monetray Sanctions Against Edward Alkhasov
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF R. R.'s Motion For Terminating And Monetray Sanctions Against Edward Alkhasov. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 175 BLUXOME HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. EMERALD CITY LOFTS BUILDING, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANTS EMERALD CITY LOFTS BUILDING,EMERALD CITY LOFTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. On a demurrer the Court looks solely to the face of the complaint. Given the policy of liberal construction, the First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support the class action allegations. In particular, it is not possible to determine simply from the allegations that common issues do not predominate. Compare Defendants' Reply at 3 line 14 et seq. rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pershing Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment *Continued Tentative Ruling Part 3 Of 3*
rulings.Rulings: Pershing Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment *Continued Tentative Ruling Part 3 Of 3*. 2. Unenforceable contract The letter signed by Nygaard and sent to Sundby via email states: "In the normal course of business, and consistent with our normal pricing protocols, we will work to forge a mutually beneficial contract for any services we ultimately provide to [P]. Please note that this letter should not be construed as [D's] (or its affiliates') agreement to financially participate in your new venture or to make any specific investment in systems development to help you in initiating your new venture. The details of any future agreements will be determined as we jointly agree to terms of any such development in contracts to be created going forward." (Alexander Decl., Ex. K, p. 3.) Read in its entirety, and specifically the above, the letter is nothing more than an agreement to agree in the future. This is unenforceable as a matter of law. See Copeland v. Baskin Robbins U.S.A. 96 Cal.App.4th 1251, 1256(2002); Bustamante v. Intuit, Inc. 141 Cal.App.4th 199, 213 (2006) (holding that because the essential terms of the contract were only sketched out, with final form to be agreed upon in future, parties had at best "agreement to agree."). The only portion of the contract that might conceivably be enforceable is the promise that "We will contact a select group of [Pershing's] clients to inquire if they would be willing to meet with you." (Alexander Decl., Ex. K, p. 3.) However, this statement is qualified by the statement that "We will agree on the appropriate approach and level of disclosure prior to making those contacts." (Id.) Even this promise is indefinite and premised on a promise to agree in the future and therefore fails. DM's Objections DM's objections to the evidence provided by Pershing are overruled. RE: Objections 1-6: Previous litigation is relevant under Kids Universe (Court may consider the experience and track record of the company's principle in determining whether lost profits were speculative); previously failed companies and description "Anti-Entrepreneur of the Year" is relevant to whether anyone would knowingly have invested in Sundby's new project. The § 352 objection in the context of a summary judgment motion is not well taken: The court can distinguish the relevance from any prejudicial effect. RE: Objection 7: the material shows a different version of the Letter Attachment was attached to the amended complaint. RE: Objections 8 and 10-13 are to various declarations and portions of deposition testimony that are not cited in Pershing's Separate Statement. The Court rejects the application of the so-called Golden Rule. "Following the discretionary approach, it may be an abuse of discretion to disregard evidence that was clearly called to the attention of the court and opposing parties but inadvertently omitted from the separate statement of undisputed facts." W. Rylaarsdam, et al., CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL §§ 10:95.4 - 10:95.5 (Rutter Group 2011). Accordingly, Objections 8 and 10-13 are overruled. RE: Objection 9: the assertedly conflicting materials only go to the weight of the objected-to evidence, not admissibility. Objections 14-16: There is no authority for the proposition that assertedly undisputed facts not specifically cited in the memorandum should be stricken. Pershing's Objections These are sustained, and in particular those made to the Graham Declaration (and Report) and the Nygaard email for reasons stated above. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT PERSHING LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted for two independent reasons. 1. Lost profits DEEPmoney (DM) only seeks lost profits. Pershing Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts ("SSUF") 3. Although DM contends it also seeks 'market value' of the company, that in turn is a function of lost profits. (DM Opposition at 17 line 8: "market value" based on income and "cash generation".) If the latter are speculative, then so is the former. DM cannot establish its damages for lost profits, failing to demonstrate a reasonable probability that profits would have been earned but for Pershing's conduct. Damages for the loss of prospective profits are recoverable where the evidence makes reasonably certain their occurrence and extent. Kids' Universe v. In2Labs, 95 Cal.App.4th 870, 883 (2002). Pershing presents evidence that Sundby is DM's founder, sole member, CEO, and arguably sole employee (SSUFs 1-2); DM never created or developed a marketable product (SSUFs 4-5, 10-12); DM never had any operating income (SSUF 6); no outside source ever invested money in DM (SSUF 8); DM needed to secure registration from the SEC and marketplace acceptance of its product in order to bring DM into an operational form (SSUF 12); DM never registered as an investment advisor or started the process to register any fund (SSUF 13); DM's arguable sole employee (Sundby) has no prior professional role in the financial services industry (arguably at best because Sundby is not paid and is not clear himself if he is an employee, Sundby deposition at 150), has a history of two failed startups, sued prior investors in his previous startups, and was named "Anti-Entrepreneur of the Year" in 1998 (SSUFs 15-22); Sundby has raised no money from any investor since 2004 (SSUF 22). This evidence would require a reasonable trier of fact to find it more likely than not that there were no lost profits. Accordingly, I turn to a review whether DM can establish a triable issue of fact on damages. I ignore of course the allegation of DM's pleadings in determining whether there is evidence to support DM's position. DM's evidence of his own investment, contacts with potential funding sources, a patent application, and the retention of a law firm and some surveys does is not enough to find a triable issue. As in Kids Universe, the scenario presented by DM is rife with speculation. The evidence shows the plan and product was at such an early stage of development that no one knew what form it would take. DM states repeatedly that it "never got that far" or was prevented from developing the product or securing investors because of Pershing's breach (e.g., SSUFs 6 and 10 and P's Response to D's SSUF 13). DM argues that if Pershing had not breached, DM would have developed the product into an operational form, secured investor funding (if necessary), secured the necessary registration from the SEC, gained marketplace acceptance and successfully recruited clients to use the product, and made a certain amount of profit. All of this is speculation, guesswork and conjecture that is impermissible under Kids Universe. ...CONTINUED.... =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DEEPMONEY LLC VS. PERSHING LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Pershing Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment *Continued Tentative Ruling Part 2 Of 3*
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 7, Pershing Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment *Continued Tentative Ruling Part 2 Of 3*. DM's reliance on Nygaard's email comments that "the five year number should be between 20 and 25 billion¿The Star net number should be about 5.5 million per month" (Sundby Decl. 15 and Ex. H) is misplaced. Regardless of whether Nygaard has the qualifications to testify as an expert (and that is not demonstrated), these comments were not his expert opinion regarding DM's market value or potential profits. Rather, Nygaard's comments were provided re: a "pro-forma financial plan that reflected the candidate CEO's most current assumptions." (Sundby Decl. 9-15.) The financial plan was drafted by Sundby. Id. Commentary on a draft financial plan including projections and assumptions is not expert opinion of future lost profits. Moreover, the financial plan was developed for the purpose of "presenting it to others who could assist [DM]." Sundby Decl. 29. This plan, based itself on assumptions and intended as a marketing tool to present to future investors, is not a sufficient foundational basis upon which to base an expert opinion even if Nygaard's comments were intended as such. See Parlour Enterprises, Inc. v. Kirin Group, Inc., (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 281, 289-290.) Accordingly, Nygaard's opinion should be disregarded and does not raise a triable issue. The Graham Declaration and Report too fail to raise triable issues. These items are not based on a sufficient factual basis. The report is based on "contemporaneous records generated by the parties' in the course of their business dealings¿academic literature, publicly available financial reports, and publicly available industry data, interest rates and financial indices¿" as well as an interview with Sundby. Graham Report, p. 3. But the report is based on what DM intended to do, not what it actually did, or what any same or similar other business did. See, e.g., Graham Report, pp. 17, 18, 22. See e.g. id. at 10 (assumes the everything would happen as planned). The report assumes that DM would have been able to come up with the initial seed capital and that DM's intentions would have become reality, without any evidence indicating that was likely to happen. In short, Graham assumes what his report is meant to establish. The general approach taken by DM to show the relative certainty of damages is to focus not on the inchoate plans and vague possibilities of DM, but rather to examine the market as a whole, the "well established mutual fund industry, with enormous profits." Opposition at 21 line 20. See generally id. at 18 et seq. It is on this basis that stunningly high numbers can be bandied about ("billion" dollars, id. at 18 line 15; or perhaps a $2.6 billion or even a trillion dollars, Sundby Deposition at 1224-1225). But this is no more persuasive than it would have been for the plaintiff in Kids' Universe to note profits in its industry. DM's argument of course proves too much, i.e., that any person considering entering an extant business line will succeed. In fact, the relevant "track record" is not of the industry one desires to enter, it is of the plaintiff and its predecessors. Compare Greenwich S.F. v. Wong, 190 Cal.App.4th 739, 763 (2010). =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: BERNARD WARD et al VS. GMAC MORTGAGE, LLC et al

rulings.Case Title: CAI XIAO SHENG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminatin Sanctions And/Or Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And/Or Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. NEIL EISENBERG, AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND AS TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 18, CROSS DEFENDANT CHICAGO TITLE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. 1st, 2d and 4th causes of action: statute of limitations: demurrers sustained with leave to amend. To the extent cross complainants are relying on the delayed discovery rule, they must plead with specificity the time and manner of discovery, and why they did not make the discovery earlier. 1st cause of action: Otherwise overruled, as the confidential relationship is sufficiently alleged ("escrow holder"). 2nd cause of action: otherwise sustained with leave to plead each element of the claim with specificity (the 'who, what, where, and when'). In this context, the court is interested solely in allegations against cross defendant, not other entities. Among other things, consequent damages must be specifically alleged, which may pose issues given the offer of Fidelity National to compensate (cross complaint 16 e). 3rd cause of action: Otherwise overruled. See discussion under first cause of action. 4th cause of action: Otherwise sustained with leave to plead each element including damages. See comments above re: damages. 6th cause of action: sustained with leave to plead appropriate facts in support of the claim. 7th cause of action: sustained with leave to plead a restitution claim, specifying among other things the nature of the restitution sought. Demurrers otherwise overruled. Cross complainants have 10 days leave to amend if they can do so in good faith. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is unopposed and is accordingly granted. Cross-Complainants' request for judicial notice is denied. Copies of a web-site are not judicially noticeable, at least not for the truth of their content. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 23, CROSS DEFENDANTS GORDON & REES LLP, ANDREW D CASTRICONE'SDEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Request for Judicial Notice, granted as unopposed. Demurrer sustained, without leave to amend. The action is time-barred. Cross-Complainants' claims all stem from Cross-Defendants' alleged legal malpractice. Therefore the statute of limitations at C.C.P. § 340.6 applies. Cross-Complainants admit that Cross-Defendants' legal representation ended on April 14, 2008. Cross-Complaint, 50. Cross-Complainants were on notice of claims not later than February 2008. Cross-Complaint, 53. Cross-Complainants filed their Cross-Complaint on March 23, 2012, which is thus time-barred. Cross-Complainants have not shown how they may plead in good faith around the statute of limitations. No tolling past April 2008 is possible. Cross-Complaint, 50. Amendment is futile. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 1, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT CHARLES LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with leave to amend, amended complaint due within 10 days. Plaintiff has not alleged facts that she was neglected within the meaning of the statute which requires "acts of egregious abuse" on a par with acts cited at e.g., Carter v. Prime Healthcare, 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 405 et seq. (2011).
